config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because that can easily become an excuse for abuse of power. In my view, a healthy society protects individual rights first, while still allowing limits only when there is a clear and necessary public good, such as safety or preventing harm to others. From my perspective, once the state or the majority can override personal rights too easily, ordinary people are the ones who suffer most.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum atas hak individu, karena bagi saya itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Dari perspektif Amerika, saya melihat hak individu sebagai batas penting terhadap negara dan mayoritas; masyarakat memang perlu aturan, tetapi hanya ketika pembatasannya benar-benar perlu untuk mencegah bahaya nyata. Kalau hak pribadi selalu bisa dikalahkan demi “kepentingan bersama” yang kabur, yang paling sering dirugikan justru orang biasa, bukan elite. Saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan yang ketat: masyarakat kuat lahir dari individu yang haknya dilindungi, bukan dari individu yang dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because that is too easy to abuse. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to “public interest” or “social harmony” can be used to pressure people into silence, and that worries me more than a little disorder from protected freedom. Society does matter, but it should justify limits on rights only in narrow cases like public safety, not as a blanket excuse to override personal dignity. A healthy society is built when individual rights are respected first, then balanced with real collective needs.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak tegas gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan atas hak individu secara umum. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat sejarah kami penuh pelajaran bahwa ketika mayoritas atau negara diberi kuasa terlalu besar atas individu, penyalahgunaan hampir selalu mengikuti, jadi hak pribadi harus jadi pagar utama. Argumen tentang “harmoni sosial” atau “kepentingan publik” memang terdengar mulia, tetapi terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam orang biasa, bukan melindungi mereka. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling disiplin membatasi kekuasaan demi melindungi kebebasan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take blanket priority over the rights of the individual, because in practice that often becomes a tool for silencing dissent and protecting power. From my Indonesian perspective, I have seen how “public interest” and “social harmony” can be used to pressure people into obedience, even when their rights are being ignored. Society does need limits on individual freedom in some cases, especially for safety and preventing harm, but those limits must be narrow and clearly justified. A good society is not one that easily sacrifices individuals; it is one that protects rights first and only restricts them when there is a real, concrete need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, pengalaman politik kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi ruang terlalu luas atas nama “kepentingan umum” atau “harmoni sosial”, penyalahgunaan kekuasaan sangat mudah terjadi dan justru orang biasa yang paling dirugikan. Hak individu harus menjadi batas utama, sementara pembatasan hanya sah jika ada bahaya nyata, jelas, dan proporsional. Masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan kebebasan, melainkan yang paling disiplin melindungi hak sambil membatasi kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
